text: Working with APIs is a valuable skill for modern developers. An API allows different software applications to communicate. When you send an HTTP request, for example a GET request to retrieve data or a POST request to send data, the API usually responds with data in JSON format. Many APIs require authentication, such as an API key, to verify your identity. You should also pay attention to rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time. Finally, always implement error handling. Sometimes requests fail due to network issues or invalid data, so your code should manage these errors gracefully without crashing.
